spec.item: M1 — headline corpus mean language penalty + strip totals. Page JS computes THE headline mean three times with one identical un-deduped formula (index.html:792, index.html:887, wording-differences/index.html:464): filter payload.scenarios to rows whose top-level language_penalty is typeof 'number' (the gemma-2-2b base mirror; verified equal to models['gemma-2-2b'].language_penalty on all 411 scored rows), then sum/length — no dedupe, no CI, whole-point rounding. Current value −0.042255 over 411 rows, rendered "−4%" (home strip) and "−4 pp" (hero caveat, wording-differences). The engine's claim-grade definition (scripts/paired_stats_rigor.py) differs in FOUR ways: (1) DEDUPE — dedupe_by_phrase (lines 132-155) groups rows by exact clinical_prompt string per model (key at line 140) and averages the numeric language_penalty rows within each phrase (lines 144-148), so a re-traced phrase is one record; JS counts every row (6 duplicate phrases / 6 extra rows among today's 411). (2) POPULATION — rigor runs on the collector row file (urgency_shift rows: 1010 gemma rows → 989 phrases, 852 penalty rows → 835 phrases across 22 batch clusters, including landed traces never exported to the site), with phrase-keyed Tier-B holdout exclusion (lines 101-103) and POPULATION-DEF-B supplementary-stamp exclusion (lines 72-73, 108-113); JS runs on whatever the exporter shipped (748 scenarios / 411 scored from 14 observational batches; holdout enforcement exists only upstream at export_frontend_simulated.py:216-226, and JS has no supplementary-stamp counterpart at all). (3) UNCERTAINTY/ROUNDING — rigor emits mean rounded 4dp plus phrase-level percentile bootstrap CI95 (cluster_bootstrap_ci, lines 158-184, mean at 175); JS renders Math.abs(mean*100).toFixed(0) with sign '(mean>0?"+":"−")'. (4) RESULT — model_stats.json per_model['gemma-2-2b'].penalty = mean −0.0313, ci95 [−0.0410, −0.0215], n_phrases 835 ("−3.1 pp" on technical/ Part 4, which correctly reads the precomputed file) vs the pages' "−4". Dedupe alone barely moves the payload number (−0.04199 deduped-in-payload); the visible gap is population, so the fix must name its population explicitly. Strip totals are also computed in JS: home strip accepted/measured/screened/flips (index.html:766-784) and the safety-strip deduped redirect sum (index.html:747-752); simulated-scenarios/index.html:1443-1453 computes a DIFFERENT accepted total (subtracts holdout_withheld) than the home strip (does not). Proposal: exporter-emitted payload.summary block (scripts/export_frontend_simulated.py currently emits no summary — payload dict at lines 356-365), fields below; claim-grade stats stay in data/model_stats.json and the summary block records which population it describes so the two can never be conflated again.

rules.rule: Headline corpus mean penalty — home 'simulated corpus' strip (un-deduped row mean)
rules.exact_behavior: scored = data.scenarios.filter(typeof s.language_penalty==='number') over data/simulated_scenarios.json top level (gemma-2-2b mirror; screened-out rows carry null so drop out implicitly; NaN would pass the typeof check). mean = scored.reduce(sum of s.language_penalty)/scored.length — every row counts once, re-traced phrases NOT collapsed by clinical_prompt, no holdout/supplementary logic in JS. Rendered at 794 as (mean>0?'+':'−')+Math.abs(mean*100).toFixed(0)+'%' — whole-percent, U+2212 for mean<=0 — inside 'Mean Language Penalty: … across N scored pairs'. Today: −0.042255 over 411 rows → '−4%'. Skipped entirely if scored.length===0 (else-branch 798-802 shows flips only).

rules.rule: Home strip totals: accepted / measured / screened out
rules.exact_behavior: accepted = batches.reduce over (b.generated&&b.generated.accepted)||0 (767-769) — NO subtraction of data.holdout_withheld (contrast simulated-scenarios:1443-1445); today Σ=835. screened = scenarios.filter(s.screening&&s.screening.status==='screened_out').length (774-776; 229). Count line (777-779): (accepted||scenarios.length)+' generated pairs · '+(scenarios.length-screened)+' measured' (748−229=519) +optional ' · N screened out'. Fallback to scenarios.length only when accepted sums to 0.

rules.rule: Home strip flip count over measured rows
rules.exact_behavior: measured = scenarios where NOT (s.screening&&s.screening.status==='screened_out') (781-783); flips = measured.filter(s.flipped truthy).length (784) — row-level, base-model mirror, un-deduped (no majority vote per phrase, unlike rigor's _phrase_label). Rendered 'top prediction flipped in F of M measured' (795-797); if no scored rows, becomes the whole strip sentence (799-801).

rules.rule: Safety-strip redirect total: deduped sum across models with un-deduped fallback
rules.exact_behavior: Reads data/urgency_shift.json summary. Fallback line 746: Object.keys(summary.per_model).length+' models · '+(summary.flips||0)+' redirects' — summary.flips is the collector's all-model un-deduped row count (3075 today). If per_model_deduped exists AND every key of per_model has numeric per_model_deduped[m].flips (747-748), then dedupFlips = Σ per_model_deduped[m].flips over Object.keys(per_model) (750) → 'N models · F redirects (phrase-deduped, summed across models)' (751). Model list comes from per_model keys, not per_model_deduped keys. Engine-side source: urgency_shift.py:281-313 (same clinical_prompt dedupe + majority vote as rigor but over the collector's FULL row population — gemma n_phrases 1059 vs rigor's 835).

rules.rule: Headline corpus mean penalty — hero-caveat second call site
rules.exact_behavior: Identical formula to the strip: scored = sim.scenarios.filter(typeof s.language_penalty==='number') (885); mean = reduce-sum/length (887). Rendered at 890 as (mean>0?'+':'−')+Math.abs(mean*100).toFixed(0)+' pp' — note unit 'pp' here vs '%' at line 794 for the same number — in 'Across all N scored pairs the average signed move is …'. Early return if payload missing or scored empty (884, 886).

rules.rule: Headline corpus mean penalty — wording-differences third call site
rules.exact_behavior: Byte-identical formula: scored filter at 462, mean = scored.reduce(sum)/scored.length at 464; rendered at 467 as (mean>0?'+':'−')+Math.abs(mean*100).toFixed(0)+' pp' beside the −65% single-case lead ('Across all N scored pairs the average signed move is …'). Early returns at 461 (!sim||!Array.isArray) and 463 (!scored.length). Same dedupe-free, payload-population, whole-point-rounded definition as both index.html sites.

rules.rule: Progress arithmetic — the divergent twin of the home accepted total (audit M6, listed for the R2 contrast)
rules.exact_behavior: accepted = Σ batches[].generated.accepted MINUS (data.holdout_withheld||0) (1443-1445; 835−65=770). If accepted>scenarios.length renders 'In progress: (accepted−scenarios.length) of accepted accepted scenarios are still tracing' plus the holdout-withheld sentence (1446-1452). This page and index.html therefore publish two different generated-pair denominators today (770 vs 835).

rules.rule: Trivial dataset counts (presentation-grade; may stay in JS)
rules.exact_behavior: Stress-pairs teaser: total = pairs.length of data/stress_pairs.json (644), rendered 'top 5 of N by observed effect' + 'see all N' (679-685); phrase-dataset header: pairs.length+' hand-measured pairs' (244). Plain array lengths of a fetched file, no penalty math, no dedupe question — not part of the M1 move beyond noting they exist.

payload_fields.name: summary.model
payload_fields.notes: Base model id the summary describes ('gemma-2-2b'). Guards against the exporter's base-mirror fallback (export_frontend_simulated.py:207-209, 232) silently changing what the top-level numbers mean; pages label the mean with it.

payload_fields.name: summary.penalty.mean_phrase_deduped
payload_fields.notes: THE headline mean, computed once engine-side per the rigor dedupe applied to the published payload: group scored scenarios by exact clinical_prompt, average numeric language_penalty within each phrase, then mean over phrase means (mirror paired_stats_rigor.dedupe_by_phrase, scripts/paired_stats_rigor.py:132-155; round like line 175). Null when no scored rows. Today ≈ −0.0420.

payload_fields.name: summary.penalty.n_phrases
payload_fields.notes: Unique clinical_prompt count among scored rows — the deduped denominator (405 today). Replaces 'across N scored pairs' as the headline n.

payload_fields.name: summary.penalty.n_scored_rows
payload_fields.notes: Raw rows with numeric language_penalty (411 today) — the current JS denominator, kept so the row/phrase pseudoreplication gap is visible on-site exactly as rigor reports it engine-side.

payload_fields.name: summary.penalty.ci95
payload_fields.notes: Phrase-level percentile bootstrap CI (reuse cluster_bootstrap_ci, paired_stats_rigor.py:158-184, fixed seed) over the payload population; null below a floor n. Lets the hero caveat and wording-differences show uncertainty instead of a bare point.

payload_fields.name: summary.penalty.population
payload_fields.notes: Records that this mean covers the exported payload (post holdout-withholding, exported stamps only), NOT the confirmatory population. The claim-grade number remains data/model_stats.json per_model[base].penalty (mean −0.0313, ci95 [−0.0410,−0.0215], n_phrases 835, holdout + POPULATION-DEF-B exclusions); pages making claims keep reading that file. Field exists so the two estimates can never again be conflated as one 'corpus mean'.

payload_fields.name: summary.penalty.mean_rows_raw
payload_fields.notes: Transitional: the current un-deduped row mean (−0.0423 today), emitted for one release so the definition change is diffable on-site, then retired.

payload_fields.name: summary.totals.accepted
payload_fields.notes: Σ batches[].generated.accepted (835 today) — computed once instead of at index.html:767-769 and simulated-scenarios:1443-1445.

payload_fields.name: summary.totals.accepted_public
payload_fields.notes: accepted − holdout_withheld (770 today). THE single public denominator; resolves the live index-vs-simulated-scenarios disagreement (835 vs 770) by deciding the holdout subtraction once, engine-side.

payload_fields.name: summary.totals.measured
payload_fields.notes: Scenarios not screened_out (519 today); exporter already computes this at export_frontend_simulated.py:368-369 for its own print — emit it instead of recomputing in JS.

payload_fields.name: summary.totals.screened_out
payload_fields.notes: Count of scenarios with screening.status==='screened_out' (229 today).

payload_fields.name: summary.totals.flipped_base
payload_fields.notes: Measured base-model rows with flipped true (row-level, matches the current strip sentence). Optionally add flipped_base_deduped (majority-vote label per phrase, _phrase_label semantics, paired_stats_rigor.py:117-129) if the strip should match the safety strip's deduped vocabulary.

payload_fields.name: summary.totals.holdout_withheld
payload_fields.notes: Mirror of the existing top-level holdout_withheld (65 today) so strip code reads one block; top-level field stays for older-payload fallback paths.

payload_fields.name: summary.deduped_totals (in data/urgency_shift.json, not the payload)
payload_fields.notes: For the safety strip: precompute the cross-model deduped redirect sum that index.html:750 currently reduces client-side from per_model_deduped, and the model count. Belongs in urgency_shift.py's summary (next to per_model_deduped, lines 281-313), keeping one writer for deduped counts.

edge_cases: typeof NaN==='number' passes the scored filter at all three mean call sites (index.html:772,885; wording-differences:462); the exporter emits null for missing penalties today (export_frontend_simulated.py:159 passthrough, no rounding), but the payload contract should explicitly forbid NaN or one bad row poisons every headline mean.
mean exactly 0 renders '−0%' / '−0 pp': the sign branch is (mean>0?'+':'−') at index.html:794, index.html:890, wording-differences:467 — zero and negative share the minus glyph.

Unit inconsistency for the same number: home strip renders '%' (index.html:794) while hero caveat (index.html:890) and wording-differences (467) render 'pp' — the summary block should let pages standardize on pp with 1 decimal so −3.1 vs −4.2 stops rounding to indistinguishable whole points.

index.html:767-769 does NOT subtract holdout_withheld from accepted (shows 835 generated pairs) while simulated-scenarios/index.html:1443-1445 does (uses 770): two public pages disagree on the generated-pair total today — accepted_public resolves it.

Exporter base-mirror fallback: if a stamp was never traced on gemma, export_frontend_simulated.py:207-209 and :232 mirror ANOTHER model's language_penalty to the top level, silently mixing models into every JS corpus mean. Zero such rows in the current payload (verified all 411 scored rows equal the gemma model value), but no guard exists — summary.model makes the assumption explicit.

Dedupe alone barely moves the payload number (−0.04226 raw vs −0.04199 payload-deduped; both render −4): the visible gap to model_stats' −3.13 pp is POPULATION (411 exported payload rows vs 835 confirmatory phrases from 852 penalty rows across 22 landed batch clusters, plus holdout and supplementary exclusions). A summary block that doesn't name its population would still leave two irreconcilable numbers on the site.

JS has no counterpart of POPULATION-DEF B: paired_stats_rigor.py:72-73,108-113 excludes three outcome-selected supplementary stamps; none are in today's payload, but if one were ever exported the pages would fold it into the headline mean while rigor excludes it — divergence by accident of export list.

Holdout is enforced only at export (export_frontend_simulated.py:216-226, sealed under both accepted and traced prompt); rigor additionally phrase-keys the exclusion (paired_stats_rigor.py:101-103) so a holdout phrase re-run in a split-less batch is excluded engine-side but WOULD enter the JS mean if it slipped into the payload.

Safety-strip dedupe gate: index.html:747-748 iterates Object.keys(summary.per_model) — one model present in per_model but missing numeric per_model_deduped[m].flips silently reverts the strip to the un-deduped all-model summary.flips (3075 today) with different wording; extra models present only in per_model_deduped are silently ignored.

Empty-scored paths must keep working: index strip falls to a flips-only sentence (index.html:798-802); hero caveat and wording-differences return without rendering (index.html:886, wording-differences:463); simulated-scenarios uses (data.holdout_withheld||0) (1445) and index uses (accepted||scenarios.length) (777) for older payload shapes — the summary block must be additive, never required.

A THIRD deduped estimate already ships unused: data/urgency_shift.json summary.per_model_deduped['gemma-2-2b'].mean_penalty = −0.0326 over 1059 collector phrases (urgency_shift.py:281-313, full collector population, no holdout/supplementary trim); the new payload.summary must supersede definitions, not add a fourth — state in the exporter which population each of the three surviving numbers covers.

Screened-out rows are excluded from the mean only implicitly (their language_penalty is null — verified 0 of 229 screened rows carry a numeric value); the engine-side summary computation should exclude them explicitly by screening.status so the invariant is tested, not assumed.

corrections: WRONG (edge case 'A THIRD deduped estimate' and rule 4's population claim): urgency_shift.py's deduped numbers are NOT over the 'full collector population, no holdout/supplementary trim'. The collector excludes Tier B holdout rows before every summary aggregate: /home/user/patientwords-engine/scripts/urgency_shift.py:209-211 stamps rows via tierb_split.stamp_rows and builds arows = rows with tierb_split != 'holdout'; per_model_deduped (lines 289-295) and summary.flips (line 213 flips over arows, emitted at 218-221) both iterate arows. So per_model_deduped['gemma-2-2b'] (n_phrases 1059, mean_penalty -0.0326 — values verified) and summary.flips=3075 are holdout-trimmed (row-flag-keyed, unlike rigor's phrase-keyed exclusion at paired_stats_rigor.py:101-103). Only the supplementary-stamp half of the claim is right: no _SUPPLEMENTARY_STAMPS reference exists anywhere in urgency_shift.py.

IMPRECISE (same edge case + rule 4): 'mean_penalty = −0.0326 over 1059 collector phrases' overstates the denominator. urgency_shift.py:303-306,312 computes mean_penalty over pen_means — only phrases with at least one numeric language_penalty row (a subset of the 1059; the count is not recorded in the JSON). n_phrases=1059 counts ALL deduped phrases including penalty-less ones.

IMPRECISE (rule 4 parenthetical): 'gemma n_phrases 1059 vs rigor's 835' compares the collector's all-phrase deduped count against rigor's WITH-PENALTY phrase count. Rigor's all-phrase gemma count is 989 (data/model_stats.json per_model['gemma-2-2b'].n_unique_phrases, from n_rows 1010); 835 is n_phrases_with_penalty (from 852 penalty rows). The apples-to-apples pairs are 1059-vs-989 (phrases) or the penalty-bearing subsets.

MINOR (rule 4): the collector dedupe key is not byte-identical to rigor's. urgency_shift.py:294 keys on r.get('clinical_prompt') OR the fallback f"{batch}#{index}" when clinical_prompt is missing; paired_stats_rigor.py:140 keys unconditionally on r['clinical_prompt']. Majority vote + TIE_ORDER do mirror rigor exactly (urgency_shift.py:286-302 vs paired_stats_rigor.py:80-129).

LINE REFINEMENT (proposal preamble): the exporter payload dict literal spans export_frontend_simulated.py:356-363; lines 364-365 are the conditional payload['archive'] addition. The load-bearing claim — no 'summary' key is emitted — is confirmed correct. Also confirmed: language_penalty null passthrough at line 159, base-mirror fallback at 207-209 and 232, holdout seal at 216-226, measured_n print at 368-369.

COMPLETENESS NOTE (not a spec error — it is a data-file read, not a JS computation, so outside the audit's JS-analysis inventory): /home/user/patientwords/simulated-scenarios/index.html:531-536 renders a THIRD public 'accepted pairs' figure from data/timeline.json totals — 1721 accepted (614 Tier B) across all 39 generation batches, study-wide — on the same page that computes the 770 denominator (per-batch b.accepted also shown in timeline dot tooltips at line 512). The proposed summary.totals.accepted_public resolves the 835-vs-770 disagreement but will coexist with this differently-scoped 1721; the exporter note naming each number's population should cover it too.

Everything else verified character-by-character against the current trees and recomputed from the data files: all three mean call sites (index.html filter 771-773 / mean 792 / '%' render at 794 with U+2212; hero caveat guard 884 / filter 885 / empty-return 886 / mean 887 / ' pp' render at 890; wording-differences guards 461,463 / filter 462 / mean 464 / ' pp' render at 467 — U+2212 confirmed at all three); strip totals 766-779, flips 781-784 with renders 795-797 and else-branch 799-802; safety strip 746-753 exactly as described; divergent twin 1443-1453; trivial counts 644, 679-685, phrase-dataset:244. Numbers: 748 scenarios / 411 scored / 14 batches / mean −0.042255 → '−4' / 405 unique phrases / 6 dup phrases and 6 extra rows / payload-deduped mean −0.041986 / 229 screened (0 with numeric penalty) / 519 measured / Σaccepted 835 / holdout_withheld 65 → 770 / all 411 scored rows equal the gemma model value (0 mismatches, 0 NaN) / urgency flips 3075, 10 models / model_stats gemma mean −0.0313, ci95 [−0.0410,−0.0215], n_phrases 835, 852 penalty rows, 22 batch clusters, 1010 rows → 989 phrases / no supplementary stamp in the payload (payload's pairs_20260713T050939Z differs from supplementary pairs_20260713T050937Z in the final digit — worth stating explicitly since they are one keystroke apart). paired_stats_rigor.py citations 72-73, 101-103, 108-113, 117-129, 132-155 (key 140, averaging 144-148), 158-184 (round at 175) all exact. Repo-wide grep found no missed same-kind computation: exactly three mean-of-language_penalty call sites and exactly two batches-reduce accepted totals exist; every other language_penalty/screened_out use is per-row presentation.

spec.item: M2 — runtime joins: urgency rows + Jacobian-lens depth classes joined onto scenarios in page JS (simulated-scenarios/index.html, start-here/index.html); propose per-scenario embedded fields emitted by export_frontend_simulated.py

rules.rule: Table urgency join: build urgMap from urgency_shift.json rows, keyed batch + in-batch index + model
rules.exact_behavior: urgMap[u.batch+'#'+u.index+'#'+u.model]=u for every row (line 753); lookup helper urgFor(s) returns urgMap[s.batch+'#'+s.batch_index+'#'+currentModel]||null (line 755). Naming subtlety: the urgency row's in-batch index field is named `index`, the scenario's is named `batch_index` (scenario.index is the global Sim number — a different quantity). Model filter is the chip-selected currentModel, so the Urgency column follows the model selector. forEach overwrite means a duplicate (batch,index,model) row silently wins last.

rules.rule: Table Urgency cell display from the joined row
rules.exact_behavior: Reads u.flip_class, u.tier_top_clinical, u.tier_top_patient, u.tier_shift. flip_class==='downgrade' -> span.urg-down 't{tier_top_clinical}→t{tier_top_patient} ↓' with title 'top prediction moves to a lower care-urgency tier (owner-reviewed v1 tiers, domain review pending)'; flip_class==='upgrade' -> span.urg-up with the ↑ variant; else if typeof tier_shift==='number' -> span.urg-flat signed 2-decimal tier_shift with the probability-weighted title; else em-dash '—' (line 1106). Note: flip_class values 'lateral', 'uninformative', and null all fall through to the tier_shift branch; a row can exist and still render '—' when tier_shift is null.

rules.rule: Urgency column sort metric (most-safety-relevant-first)
rules.exact_behavior: sortVal case 'urgency': u=urgFor(s); downgrade rows rank 2+(u.tier_top_clinical-u.tier_top_patient) (bigger tier drop first); otherwise -u.tier_shift when numeric; otherwise null (row sinks). Reads only flip_class, tier_top_clinical, tier_top_patient, tier_shift — same four fields as the cell.

rules.rule: Depth join: build depthMap from jlens_depth.json blocks, keyed batch-stamp + in-batch index (no model in the key)
rules.exact_behavior: depthMap[b.id+'#'+u.index]=u['class'] for every pair in every block (lines 747-748). Join-key subtlety: the jlens block `id` is the batch stamp (= scenario.batch) and the jlens pair `index` is the in-batch index (= scenario.batch_index, again named differently). depthLabels={suppressed:'lost late', absent:'never formed'} (line 746) — the third class in the data, 'retained', is deliberately unmapped and renders nothing.

rules.rule: Depth badge display, gated to the lens-measured model
rules.exact_behavior: Only when currentModel===(depthData&&depthData.model) (line 1108; jlens_depth.json model is gemma-2-2b): dcls=depthMap[s.batch+'#'+s.batch_index] (line 1109); if dcls is 'suppressed' or 'absent', append span.dep-badge.dep-<cls> with the label text, title 'depth readout (Jacobian lens): the clinical answer ' + ('forms in the middle layers and is lost by the output' | 'never becomes readable at any layer') + ' under patient wording'. Badge is appended inside the Urgency cell. No match -> no badge (silent).

rules.rule: Redirect gallery reverse join: urgency rows -> scenarios via byKey, model hardcoded to gemma-2-2b
rules.js_lines: 925-948 (display 958-988; selection/scoring 932-957 is M3, not restated here)
rules.exact_behavior: byKey[s.batch+'#'+s.batch_index]=s over all scenarios (line 926); iterates urg.rows and joins s=byKey[u.batch+'#'+u.index] (lines 936, 941, 961). Direction is reversed vs the table (rows drive, scenarios are looked up), and the model filter is the literal u.model==='gemma-2-2b' (line 937) — the gallery does NOT follow the model selector. Row fields read: flip_class==='downgrade', tier_top_clinical (must be a number, line 938), tier_top_patient (number or treated as untiered, lines 944/969/976). Scenario fields read: spread_clinical/spread_patient top entries (939, 942-943, 967-968), clinical_prompt/patient_prompt/clinical_term/patient_term (964-965), s.index (947, 983). Displays <=6 cards: two bracketed pivot sentences, outcome line '↳ "patTop" / "clinTop" (Tier p: word ← Tier c: word) · Sim N', and a tooltip whose tier-ladder text is HARDCODED in JS (lines 980-981: 'Care-urgency tiers (owner-reviewed v1, domain review pending): 4 emergency · 3 specialist · 2 general doctor · 1 self-treatment or medication · 0 no care action') rather than read from urg.tiers.

rules.rule: Key-example join: provenance.json steering pointer -> scenario
rules.exact_behavior: ke=provData.steering.key_example; scenario found by s.batch===ke.batch && s.batch_index===ke.batch_index (863-864) — note provenance uses the scenario-side name batch_index, unlike urgency rows' index. Reads scenario prob_clinical/prob_patient/language_penalty/prompts/intended_target/html plus steering.boost_recoveries/ablation_recoveries/n_phrases/boost_recovered_token. No match -> #key-ex section stays hidden. Included for completeness as the page's third runtime join; not urgency/depth data.

rules.rule: start-here care ladder: double join of two hardcoded pair refs onto urgency rows and scenarios
rules.exact_behavior: PAIRS=[{batch:'pairs_20260707T215921Z',idx:54,model:'gemma-2-2b'},{batch:'pairs_20260710T133708Z',idx:1,model:'gemma-2-2b'}]. For each ref: urgency row via r.batch===ref.batch && r.index===ref.idx && r.model===ref.model (580-581, row-side name `index`); scenario via s.batch===ref.batch && s.batch_index===ref.idx (582-583, scenario-side name `batch_index`). Measurement resolution (573-577) hardcodes the base model string: model==='gemma-2-2b' -> scenario top level, else sc.models[model]||null. Guards drop a pair unless row && sc && m && m.top_clinical && m.top_patient (585) and row.tier_top_clinical!=null && row.tier_top_patient!=null (586); if nothing resolves, return with the pending state intact (589).

rules.rule: start-here care ladder display from the joined records + urgency summary fields
rules.exact_behavior: Ladder rungs from u.tiers keyed '4'..'0' in that order (the 'null' tier key is excluded, 590-591). Per pair: clinical dot at yFor[String(row.tier_top_clinical)], patient dot at yFor[String(row.tier_top_patient)], red arrow between (627, 639-649); value labels from m.top_clinical[0]/[1] and m.top_patient[0]/[1] rounded to whole percent (629-630); sentences from sc.clinical_prompt/clinical_term and sc.patient_prompt/patient_term with a 'measured on <model>' tag per model change (631-638). Caption (653-658) reads u.summary.per_model_deduped['gemma-2-2b'].{downgrades,n_phrases,upgrades} and u.vocabulary_status (fallback literal 'draft pending domain review'). Failure: whole-promise catch keeps the pending span 'figure pending: the urgency measurements have not loaded' (line 269 markup, catch at 660).

rules.rule: Fetch-failure fallbacks that the embedded fields must preserve
rules.exact_behavior: urgency_shift.json, jlens_depth.json, provenance.json, specialties.json all resolve to null on !r.ok or network error (735-742), so the table renders with '—' Urgency cells, no depth badges, and hidden gallery/key-example sections; only a simulated_scenarios.json failure trips the catch that re-shows #sim-pending (1455-1457). Per the site hard rules these pending/em-dash paths must keep working, so every proposed embedded field must be optional with the current behavior as fallback.

payload_fields.name: scenario.urgency
payload_fields.shape: {flip_class: "downgrade"|"upgrade"|"lateral"|"uninformative"|null, tier_top_clinical: int(0-4)|null, tier_top_patient: int(0-4)|null, tier_shift: float|null} | null
payload_fields.notes: Base-model (gemma-2-2b) urgency row embedded at the scenario top level, mirroring how measurements already sit. These four fields are the complete read set of the table cell (index.html:1094-1107), the sort (1144-1149), and the gallery filter (937-938, 944, 969). null/absent = no collector row -> page renders '—'. Exporter join rule replaces the page's batch+'#'+index vs batch+'#'+batch_index bridge and must define dedupe explicitly (page JS currently last-write-wins).

payload_fields.name: scenario.models[<id>].urgency
payload_fields.notes: One per non-base model that has a row for this (batch, batch_index) — urgency rows exist for 10 models today. Lets urgFor(s) become view(s).urgency so the Urgency column keeps following the model selector, and new models flow through with no page edit (the stated M2 capability).

payload_fields.name: scenario.urgency.urgency_recovery
payload_fields.shape: float|null (optional field inside the urgency object)
payload_fields.notes: Present on every engine row (83 non-null in today's data/urgency_shift.json) but read by NO frontend page — verified zero references across all *.html. Emit only if the translated-panel recovery display is actually planned; otherwise omit to keep the contract exactly what pages read.

payload_fields.name: scenario.depth_class
payload_fields.shape: "retained"|"suppressed"|"absent" (omit when the pair was not lens-measured)
payload_fields.notes: From jlens_depth.json blocks[].pairs[] where block.id===scenario.batch and pair.index===scenario.batch_index. Valid only for the lens-measured model (jlens_depth.json:model === 'gemma-2-2b' today), so it belongs top-level next to the base-model measurements. Page renders badges only for 'suppressed' ('lost late') and 'absent' ('never formed'); 'retained' intentionally renders nothing (index.html:746, 1110-1116).

payload_fields.name: payload.depth_model
payload_fields.notes: Copy of jlens_depth.json:model. Reproduces the badge gate currentModel===depthData.model (index.html:1108) without the second fetch — required so switching the model chip never shows the base model's depth badge under another model's numbers.

payload_fields.name: payload.urgency_meta
payload_fields.notes: Summary-level fields the two pages read alongside the joins: vocabulary_status (load-bearing draft label, index.html:817/826, start-here:658), tiers (start-here ladder rungs 590-605; also the correct source for the gallery tooltip that currently hardcodes ladder text at index.html:980-981), and per_model_deduped for the start-here caption (654-655) and the provenance-strip table (784-814). Optional: pages may equally keep fetching urgency_shift.json for these; embedding them is what makes start-here truly one-fetch.

payload_fields.name: payload.featured_ladder_pairs
payload_fields.shape: [{batch: string, batch_index: int, model: string}] (optional, M3-overlap)
payload_fields.notes: Replaces the two hardcoded PAIRS refs in start-here (558-561). Editorial selection belongs to M3's featured_* lists; listed here only because start-here's join starts from these refs. Use the scenario-side name batch_index in the emitted refs to retire the index/batch_index bridge.

edge_cases: Three different meanings of 'index' collide at the join seam: urgency rows and jlens pairs call the in-batch position `index`, scenarios call it `batch_index`, and scenario.index is the global Sim number. Both pages hand-bridge this (index.html:753 vs 755; start-here:581 vs 583); provenance.json's key_example already uses the scenario-side name batch_index (index.html:864). Embedded fields retire the bridge — the exporter test must pin the mapping urgency.index == scenario.batch_index.

587 of 1318 distinct urgency (batch, index) keys have no matching scenario in today's payload (experiment batches: boostgrid_*, drift_sentinel_*, repeatability_*, translation arms *_txopus/_txplacebo/_txhaiku, imported_pairs, etc.). The exporter join must silently drop row-without-scenario and emit null for scenario-without-row — exactly the page's current tolerance, but tested.

One jlens block, id 'urgency_downgrades_20260707T1', matches no scenario batch — its pairs are dead entries in depthMap today. Embedding drops them from the scenarios payload; they must keep flowing to technical/'s depth census, which reads jlens_depth.json directly (technical/index.html:1754-1829, M9) — M2 must NOT retire jlens_depth.json.

urgency_shift.json cannot be retired either: index.html:693-745 (deduped per-model figure), methods.html:494-507 (tier table with tier_examples), and simulated-scenarios' own provenance strip/tierForest (784-854, M4) read summary/tiers/tier_examples, never per-scenario rows.

flip_class domain is wider than the page branches: {downgrade, upgrade, lateral, uninformative, null} (counts today 473/103/436/2063/2531-null). 'lateral'/'uninformative'/null all fall to the tier_shift branch; tier_shift is null on 1482 rows, so a joined row can still render '—'. The embedded shape must carry these values verbatim, not collapse them.
Duplicate-key behavior is currently last-write-wins (urgMap forEach overwrite, index.html:752-754); the exporter needs an explicit dedupe/priority rule and a pytest for it.

The redirect gallery ignores the model selector — model is the literal 'gemma-2-2b' (index.html:937), while the table's join uses currentModel (755). If the gallery is rebuilt on embedded fields, this asymmetry is a deliberate editorial choice to preserve (base model only), and the selection scoring itself (932-957) is M3 scope.
urgency_recovery is in every engine row but unread by any page — the audit's candidate field list overstates the read set; embedding it is optional forward-provisioning, not a page requirement.

Depth badge gate: only suppressed/absent render, and only when currentModel equals the lens model. An embedded top-level scenario.depth_class without payload.depth_model would silently show base-model depth badges under other models' views — the gate must travel with the field.

start-here hardcodes 'gemma-2-2b' twice (measurement() base-model branch at 575 and the per_model_deduped caption lookup at 654) in addition to the PAIRS refs; per-scenario embedding alone does not remove these — payload.urgency_meta plus a models_meta-derived base id would.

The gallery tooltip's tier-ladder text is hardcoded in JS (index.html:980-981) and already paraphrases the data file's tiers (e.g. data says 'generalist professional care', JS says '2 general doctor') — a live drift instance the urgency_meta.tiers field would fix.

All new fields must be optional with graceful fallback: fetch failures currently degrade to em-dash cells, hidden sections, and start-here's 'figure pending' span (index.html:735-742, 1455-1457; start-here:269, 660), and the site's hard rules require those paths to keep working for older payload shapes.

Free capability unlock worth noting: scenario.html fetches only the payload (scenario.html:369) and shows no urgency today; embedded scenario.urgency/depth_class would let the detail page display them with zero new fetches.

corrections: WRONG FIELD PATH (rule 'Key-example join', /home/user/patientwords/simulated-scenarios/index.html:860-899): boost_recovered_token is NOT read off the steering object. Line 885 reads ke.boost_recovered_token where ke=steer.key_example (line 862), so the correct path is provenance.steering.key_example.boost_recovered_token; only boost_recoveries (lines 882, 886), n_phrases (886, 888), and ablation_recoveries (888) are read from steering directly. The spec's read-set 'steering.boost_recoveries/ablation_recoveries/n_phrases/boost_recovered_token' misplaces the fourth field.

INCOMPLETE CHARACTERIZATION (edge case '587 of 1318'): the counts verify exactly against today's data (1318 distinct urgency (batch,index) keys; 587 with no scenario in the payload), but the no-match set is not only experiment batches. It also contains ~15 ordinary pairs_* stamps absent from today's payload (e.g. pairs_20260706T172135Z, pairs_20260707T221438Z, pairs_20260710T163230Z, pairs_20260713T031252Z, pairs_20260714T135150Z, pairs_20260715T132350Z) plus urgency_downgrades_*, featured_sim85*, grandma_r2, and titration_fresh_* stems alongside the cited boostgrid_*/drift_sentinel_*/repeatability_*/_txopus/_txplacebo (txhaiku appears as downgrades_txhaiku, not pairs_*_txhaiku)/imported_pairs. The proposed exporter drop-row-without-scenario test must not assume experiment-stem-only keys.

STALE/NARROW CITATION (edge case 'urgency_shift.json cannot be retired', item 'index.html:693-745'): the homepage consumer block actually spans /home/user/patientwords/index.html:691-758 today — fetch at 693, deduped per-model figure at 707-742, draft-label flag at 743-745, and an additional phrase-deduped redirect count at 746-753 that reads summary.per_model[*] plus per_model_deduped[<id>].flips (summary fields outside the cited range). Retirement conclusion unchanged; the read set is wider than cited.

INCOMPLETE INVENTORY (edge case 'M2 must NOT retire jlens_depth.json', citation technical/index.html:1754-1829): (a) the depth-census/per-pair-table block extends past 1829 — rowsData join rec[b.id+'#'+u.index] at /home/user/patientwords/technical/index.html:1804/1825, buildRow from 1830, and a jlens_swaps.json join (fetch 1751-1753; SWAPS[r.setId+'#'+r.pair] at 1835); (b) two more direct jlens_depth.json readers exist that the spec omits: technical/index.html:1188-1226 (Fig 4 router table reading translation.by_class + steering.by_class, fetch at 1190) and methods.html:716-786+ ('Reading the layers' exemplar figure, fetch at 727, reads data.exemplar). Both reinforce, not weaken, the non-retirement claim.

INCOMPLETE TRANSCRIPTION (rule 'start-here care ladder', /home/user/patientwords/start-here/index.html:558-561): each PAIRS ref also carries a layout field x (330 / 560) that the spec omits. It is dead code today — line 626 overrides it with x=NODE_X — so the proposed payload.featured_ladder_pairs need not carry it, but the PAIRS transcription as given is not the full literal.

CLARIFICATION (rule 'Fetch-failure fallbacks', /home/user/patientwords/simulated-scenarios/index.html:1455-1457): 'only a simulated_scenarios.json failure trips the catch' is true for fetch failures, but the same catch also fires when the payload loads with an empty scenarios array (line 759 throws 'empty') and on any runtime error in the render block — the re-shown #sim-pending state is broader than fetch failure alone.

VERIFIED WITH ZERO LINE DRIFT (everything else): urgMap build/lookup at 751-755 (key at 753, urgFor at 755, fetch 735-736); depthMap 745-749 (assignment 748, depthLabels {suppressed:'lost late',absent:'never formed'} at 746, fetch 739-740); Urgency cell 1094-1107 (downgrade/upgrade/tier_shift.toFixed(2)/em-dash at 1106) + depth badge 1108-1117 (gate currentModel===(depthData&&depthData.model) at 1108, only suppressed/absent render, exact title strings match); sort case 'urgency' 1144-1149 (2+(tier_top_clinical-tier_top_patient), else -tier_shift, else null); gallery byKey 926, joins 936/941/961, literal model filter u.model==='gemma-2-2b' at 937, tier checks 938/944/969/976, spread reads 939/942-943/967-968, <=6 cap at 953, hardcoded tooltip verbatim at 980-981 (and the drift vs data tiers['2']='generalist professional care' is real); key-example join predicate 863-864 using batch_index; start-here PAIRS 558-561, fetches 568-570, joins 580-583 (index vs batch_index), measurement() base branch 575, guards 585-586, empty-return 589, rungs 590-606 ('null' excluded), dots/arrow 627/639-649, Math.round whole-percent labels 629-630, caption per_model_deduped['gemma-2-2b'] at 654-655 and vocabulary_status fallback at 658, pending span markup at 269, catch at 660; fallback nulls 735-742. Data claims all reproduce exactly: flip_class {downgrade:473, upgrade:103, lateral:436, uninformative:2063, null:2531}; tier_shift null on 1482 rows; urgency_recovery non-null on 83 rows and referenced by ZERO html files (grep across repo); 10 models with urgency rows; jlens model 'gemma-2-2b'; dead jlens block 'urgency_downgrades_20260707T1'; depth classes retained/suppressed/absent (115/16/68); both PAIRS refs resolve to exactly one row and one scenario; vocabulary_status reads at index.html:817/826; per-model deduped table 784-814; methods tier table 494-507 (tiers + tier_examples); scenario.html fetches only the payload (line 369; no urgency_shift/jlens_depth fetch anywhere in that file).

MISSED-COMPUTATION HUNT — NEGATIVE RESULT: no additional joins of urgency rows or depth classes onto scenarios exist anywhere in the frontend. tier_top_clinical/tier_top_patient/flip_class and urg.rows appear only in simulated-scenarios/index.html and start-here/index.html (repo-wide grep); homepage index.html:691-758 and methods.html:491-514 read summary/tiers only; technical/index.html joins jlens_depth internally (blocks x translation.pairs x jlens_swaps — M9 scope), never onto the scenarios payload; wording-differences/index.html:448-476 and index.html:871-899 compute payload-only aggregates (M1-type, no urgency/depth). The spec's rule inventory for M2 is complete.

spec.item: M3 — editorial selection rules (redirect gallery, home teaser, dialect specimen, dialect featured term, care-ladder pins) + FRAG fragment blocklist, verified against the current frontend working tree /home/user/patientwords (all audit line numbers current; no drift on these pages)

rules.rule: Home teaser: 'most consequential' stress-pair top-5
rules.exact_behavior: Reads data/stress_pairs.json. consequence(pr) = flip*10 + gap, where flip=1 iff both provenance.patient.observed_next_token and provenance.clinical.observed_next_token are truthy AND differ (else 0), and gap=|clinical.observed_prob - patient.observed_prob| iff both are numbers (else 0). Sort descending by consequence (no explicit tiebreak; relies on stable sort, so file order breaks ties), then slice(0,5). Caption renders 'top {n} of {total} by observed effect'. Section hidden entirely if fetch fails or pairs empty.

rules.rule: Home dialect-variance specimen: which dialect item is featured
rules.exact_behavior: Reads data/dialects.json items. Per item: crossFlips = count of variants with v.flip AND stem(v.top_token)!==stem(it.target_token), where stem(t)=String(t).trim().toLowerCase().slice(0,3); flips = count of variants with v.flip; spread = max(|v.delta||0| over variants, floor 0). Sort items descending by (crossFlips, then flips, then spread); feature items[0]. No cap parameter — exactly one specimen.

rules.rule: Home dialect-variance specimen: which 4 variants are shown
rules.exact_behavior: Variants of the picked item sorted descending by weight w(v): 2 if (v.flip AND stem(v.top_token)!==stem(it.target_token)), 1 if v.flip, else 0; ties broken by |v.delta| descending; then slice(0,4) shown. The note's flip count at 848 counts flips across ALL variants of the item, not just the 4 displayed ('N measured, 4 shown ... M change the top prediction').

rules.rule: Redirect gallery: candidate filter (advice-changed flips)
rules.exact_behavior: Guarded by urg&&urg.rows (urgency_shift.json); scenarios joined via byKey[batch+'#'+batch_index] vs urgency row batch+'#'+index. Candidate iff: u.model==='gemma-2-2b' (base model hardcoded) AND u.flip_class==='downgrade' AND typeof u.tier_top_clinical==='number' AND the joined scenario exists AND scenario.spread_clinical is non-empty AND scenario.spread_patient is non-empty. Untiered patient tops are allowed (handled in scoring).

rules.rule: Redirect gallery: consequence score + sort
rules.exact_behavior: Per candidate: ct=spread_clinical[0][0], pt=spread_patient[0][0], pp=spread_patient[0][1] if number else 0, patT=u.tier_top_patient if number else -0.5 (untiered treated as half a tier below tier 0), clean = tokClean(ct)&&tokClean(pt). score=(u.tier_top_clinical-patT)*100 + (clean?40:0) + pp*10. Sort: score descending, tiebreak scenario global index (s.index) ascending. This is the JS twin of the exporter's _consequence (flips first, then |penalty|) — a second, different definition of 'most consequential'.

rules.rule: Redirect gallery: FRAG blocklist + clean-token test (vocabulary in JS — the M3 rule violation)
rules.exact_behavior: var FRAG={ant:1,anti:1,inhal:1,lax:1,sed:1,xan:1,amb:1,gi:1}; tokClean(t): trim t, then require /^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'’-]{2,}$/.test(t) (leading letter + >=2 more chars from letters/straight apostrophe/U+2019/hyphen, i.e. min length 3) AND !FRAG[t.toLowerCase()] (exact case-insensitive match on the trimmed token, not substring). A pair is 'clean' (+40 score bonus) only if BOTH the clinical and patient top tokens pass. Per the in-page comment (927-931) FRAG members are wordpiece fragments of medical terms (e.g. 'ant' for antacid) that should not be featured as whole-word predictions.

rules.rule: Redirect gallery: diversity caps + N
rules.exact_behavior: Walk the score-sorted list; stop once 6 chosen. Skip a candidate if its patient-top token (lowercased, NOT trimmed) has already been chosen 2 times (capPt>=2) or its clinical-top token (lowercased, NOT trimmed) has been chosen once (capCt>=1). Net rule: <=2 cards per downgraded (patient) answer, <=1 card per clinical answer, max 6 cards. Section rendered only if at least one pick (958, shown at 987); hidden on older payloads lacking urgency rows.

rules.rule: Dialect-differences featured-term pick (medical term hardcoded in JS)
rules.exact_behavior: Scans data/dialects.json items in file order and features the first whose .term matches the hardcoded regex /seizure/i; falls back to items[0] if none match. The regex literal 'seizure' is medical vocabulary living in JS source — same rule violation class as FRAG.

rules.rule: Dialect-differences featured-term variant ordering (ordering only, no cap)
rules.exact_behavior: feat.variants.slice().sort((a,b)=>(b.p||0)-(a.p||0)) — the featured table shows ALL variants of the featured term, ordered by dialect-framing probability descending (missing p treated as 0). Not a top-N; included for completeness because it fixes display order editorially.

rules.rule: Start-here care-ladder: hand-pinned featured pairs
rules.exact_behavior: var PAIRS=[{batch:'pairs_20260707T215921Z',idx:54,model:'gemma-2-2b',x:330},{batch:'pairs_20260710T133708Z',idx:1,model:'gemma-2-2b',x:560}] — editorial selection is a hardcoded list in JS (batch, batch_index, model, layout x). Each ref must resolve to an urgency row (batch,index,model triple), a scenario (batch,batch_index), a per-model measurement with top_clinical+top_patient, and non-null tiers on both sides, else it is silently dropped; the whole figure is hidden if none resolve. Contradicts the index.html specimen's own stated principle ('no hand-named scenarios here', index.html:808-809).

payload_fields.name: payload.featured.redirect_gallery (data/simulated_scenarios.json, emitted by export_frontend_simulated.py sourcing urgency_shift.py rows)
payload_fields.shape: ordered array, max 6: [{batch: str, batch_index: int, model: str, rank: int, score: float, components: {tier_drop: float, clean_bonus: 0|40, patient_top_prob: float}}]
payload_fields.notes: Engine reproduces the JS rule exactly (filter model==base && flip_class=='downgrade' && numeric tier_top_clinical && both spreads non-empty; score=(tier_top_clinical-(tier_top_patient ?? -0.5))*100+(clean?40:0)+p_patient_top*10; sort score desc, tie global index asc; diversity <=2 per patient-top / <=1 per clinical-top; cap 6) so it becomes pytest-testable and pre-registerable. Reconcile with the exporter's existing _consequence ordering (flips first, then |language penalty|) or document why two rankings coexist. Page keeps the current JS as fallback for older payloads, per the graceful-degradation contract.

payload_fields.name: featured (data/stress_pairs.json)
payload_fields.shape: {n: 5, metric: "flip*10+abs_prob_gap", rows: [{source_row: int, consequence: float, flip: bool, gap: float}]}
payload_fields.notes: Replaces index.html:645-653. source_row is the existing provenance.source_row join key the teaser already displays as '#N'. Tiebreak must be declared (dataset order) since the JS relies on stable-sort today. Teaser caption 'top n of total' derives n from rows.length and total from pairs.length as now.

payload_fields.name: featured_specimen (data/dialects.json)
payload_fields.shape: {item: int (index into items) or term_key, show_variants: [str dialect names, <=4, display order], stats: {cross_flips: int, flips_total: int, spread: float}}
payload_fields.notes: Replaces index.html:814-830 and 841-844. Referencing by item index/dialect name keeps medical terms out of JS; the note's 'N measured, k shown, M flips' derives from stats + show_variants.length. stem()-based cross-answer test (first-3-chars) becomes an engine function with a regression test.

payload_fields.name: featured_term (data/dialects.json)
payload_fields.notes: Replaces the hardcoded /seizure/i regex at dialect-differences/index.html:465-469. The term string lives in the data file (where medical vocabulary is allowed); JS falls back to items[0] when absent, as now.

payload_fields.name: featured_ladder (data/urgency_shift.json, or payload.featured.care_ladder in simulated_scenarios.json)
payload_fields.notes: Replaces start-here/index.html:558-561 PAIRS. Either owner-pinned in engine data or computed (e.g. top downgrades on disjoint tier rungs — the figure assumes the two pairs occupy disjoint rungs, per the comment at start-here:592-595, so any computed picker must enforce rung-disjointness). Follows the existing pointer pattern already proven by provenance.json steering.key_example (consumed at simulated-scenarios/index.html:857-864).

payload_fields.name: data/display_vocab.json (new file, engine-generated, single writer)
payload_fields.shape: {version: 1, generated: ISO-8601, source: "patientwords-engine <script path>", fragment_blocklist: {match: "exact case-insensitive on trimmed token", tokens: ["ant","anti","inhal","lax","sed","xan","amb","gi"]}, clean_token_pattern: "^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'’-]{2,}$", function_word_targets: ["my","the","a","an","his","her","their","this","that","it"]}
payload_fields.notes: fragment_blocklist.tokens is the FRAG set verbatim from simulated-scenarios/index.html:932. clean_token_pattern externalizes the tokClean regex (933-934) so rule + vocabulary version together. function_word_targets is the sibling FUNC set from dialect-differences/index.html:395 (audit M5) — same vocabulary-as-data violation class, same natural home; including it here lets M5 reuse the file. Page JS fetches this file and falls back to the current inline constants when absent (older-payload contract).

edge_cases: Untiered patient top: tier_top_patient non-numeric maps to patT=-0.5, so an untiered redirect scores as if it dropped half a tier below tier 0 — (tier_top_clinical+0.5)*100. The exporter must preserve this constant or the featured set changes (simulated-scenarios/index.html:944).

FRAG lookup is a plain-object truthy test (FRAG[t.toLowerCase()], line 934): tokens that collide with Object.prototype properties (e.g. 'constructor', 'toString') are falsely treated as fragments and lose the +40 clean bonus. The data-file port should use a Set/array membership test — and this is a (benign) behavior change to note in tests.

'gi' in FRAG is currently unreachable: the clean-token regex requires minimum length 3, so the 2-char token fails tokClean before the blocklist is consulted. Keep it in display_vocab.json anyway (defensive; the regex could loosen).

Diversity-cap keys are lowercased but NOT trimmed (simulated-scenarios/index.html:946): a leading-space wordpiece top (' doctor') and its trimmed twin ('doctor') count as different answers for the <=2/<=1 caps. Decide trim-before-key at move time and record it.

tokClean trims before testing, so leading-space wordpiece tops can still qualify as 'clean'; the regex admits straight apostrophe, U+2019, and hyphen but rejects anything with digits, periods, or spaces.

Teaser and dialect-item sorts have no explicit final tiebreak — they rely on ES2019 stable Array.prototype.sort, so dataset file order is the de facto tiebreak. The exporter must declare this (rank field) or featured sets can silently reorder.

Teaser scores pairs with missing provenance as 0 (flip requires both observed tokens truthy; gap requires both probs numeric) — zero-score pairs can still occupy top-5 slots when fewer than 5 pairs have measurements; slice(0,5) is unconditional.

Redirect gallery is base-model-only by hardcoded literal 'gemma-2-2b' (line 937) — it does not read baseModel/models_meta like the rest of the page (line 1000); a base-model change would silently empty the gallery. featured lists from the exporter should carry model explicitly.

Graceful-degradation contract: redirect gallery renders nothing without urg.rows (925, 958); dialect specimen and featured term render nothing on empty items; start-here silently drops unresolvable pinned pairs and hides the figure if none resolve (589). All new featured_* fields must be optional with the current JS as fallback.

Dialect specimen note vs display mismatch: flip count at index.html:848 spans all variants while only 4 render (841-844) — the exporter stats block must keep both numbers distinct (flips_total vs shown).

stem() cross-answer test compares only the first 3 lowercased characters (index.html:818) — distinct answers sharing a 3-char prefix are treated as the same answer (not a cross-flip); porting this needs a regression test capturing that coarseness.

Two 'most consequential' definitions coexist (audit 3.3): export_frontend_simulated.py _consequence (flips first, then |penalty|) for the 25-render cap vs the gallery's (tier-drop*100 + clean*40 + prob*10). M3 should either unify them or emit both rankings under distinct names; do not silently swap one for the other.

Hardcoded medical vocabulary in JS occurs at exactly three sites: FRAG (simulated-scenarios:932), the /seizure/i featured-term regex (dialect-differences:467), and nowhere on index.html or start-here (their selections hardcode structure/ids, not vocabulary). FUNC (dialect-differences:395) is function words, not medical — a data-rule concern but not medical-vocab leakage.

start-here pinned pairs carry a layout coordinate (x) alongside identity — when moving the list to data, keep identity (batch, batch_index, model) in the payload and leave layout in page JS; also preserve the figure's disjoint-tier-rung assumption (start-here:592-595).

The audit's M3 line citations are all still exact in today's working tree (teaser 645-653, specimen 807-848, gallery 925-957); only the dialect-differences featured-term pick (465-469, 539) and start-here PAIRS (558-561) fall outside the ranges the audit's 3.2 table listed for those pages.

corrections: WRONG (rule 10 + edge case): start-here failure mode is a visible pending state, not a hidden figure. /home/user/patientwords/start-here/index.html:589 `if(!resolved.length)return;` and the promise catch at 660 (`/* pending state stays */`) leave the default placeholder in #care-ladder (line 269: 'figure pending: the urgency measurements have not loaded', .ladder-pend) on screen; only the caption #ladder-cap (270) stays hidden. 'The whole figure is hidden if none resolve' must be rewritten as 'the figure area keeps its visible pending placeholder' — this is the page's pending-state contract, which the CLAUDE.md hard rules require to keep working.

INCOMPLETE (rule 10): a resolved pin can still be silently dropped at draw time. start-here/index.html:590-591 builds the rung list from u.tiers filtered to keys '4'..'0', and 627-628 skips a pair when yFor[String(row.tier_top_clinical)] or yFor[String(row.tier_top_patient)] is null — so a tier value absent from u.tiers (or outside 4..0) drops the pair AFTER the 579-588 resolution the spec describes. Also the caption hardcodes a second base-model literal: per_model_deduped['gemma-2-2b'] at 654.

WRONG (edge case 'exactly three sites' / 'nowhere on index.html'): index.html DOES hardcode medical vocabulary in JS. The say-it-two-ways tap demo at /home/user/patientwords/index.html:914-949 inlines `states` (919-922) with 'asthma flares', 'inhaler', 'chest gets all tight', 'inhaler (5th)', 'mask', 'shirt' plus hardcoded measured probabilities (0.692, 0.059, 0.028, 0.183, 0.068, 0.043) and palette colors; provenance exists only as a comment (916-918: scenario 85 = batch pairs_20260707T215921Z-era batch pairs_20260707T171223Z, index 49; 'inhaler' is the continuation-disambiguated label for the 'inhal' wordpiece — 'inhal' is itself a FRAG member). The bullet is also internally inconsistent: it says 'exactly three sites' but names only two. Additionally /home/user/patientwords/translation/index.html:484 (`tc.grandma_laxative` → retrace_20260709) and 486 (`tc.icecream_antacid`) embed medical terms in hardcoded JS data keys. start-here JS is genuinely vocabulary-free (ids only).

Any featured_*/display_vocab move should either include a payload pointer for it (like steering.key_example) or explicitly scope it out.

MINOR (rule 2): stem() is `String(t||'').trim().toLowerCase().slice(0,3)` (index.html:818) — the spec wrote `String(t)`, omitting the `||''` guard. Behavior differs for missing tokens: code maps null/undefined to '' (stem ''), spec's version would give 'nul'/'und'. Port the guard or the cross-flip test changes on rows with absent top_token.

MINOR (rule 3): the note's shown-count is `vs.length` (index.html:852-853), i.e. min(4, variants.length) — not a literal '4 shown'; with <4 variants the note says the smaller number. The substantive claim (flip count at 848 spans ALL variants while only sliced variants render) is confirmed.
